## Constants

<a name="DefaultPrefix"></a>

```go
const (
    // DefaultPrefix is the default prefix to use for the key in the store.
    DefaultPrefix = "limiter"

    // DefaultMaxRetry is the default maximum number of key retries under
    // race condition (mainly used with database-based stores).
    DefaultMaxRetry = 3

    // DefaultCleanUpInterval is the default time duration for cleanup.
    DefaultCleanUpInterval = 30 * time.Second
)
```

## Variables

<a name="DefaultIPv4Mask"></a>

```go
var (
    // DefaultIPv4Mask defines the default IPv4 mask used to obtain user IP.
    DefaultIPv4Mask = net.CIDRMask(32, 32)
    // DefaultIPv6Mask defines the default IPv6 mask used to obtain user IP.
    DefaultIPv6Mask = net.CIDRMask(128, 128)
)
```

<a name="GetIP"></a>
## func [GetIP](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/network.go#L52>)

```go
func GetIP(r *http.Request, options ...Options) net.IP
```

GetIP returns IP address from request. If options is defined and either TrustForwardHeader is true or ClientIPHeader is defined, it will lookup IP in HTTP headers. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="GetIPWithMask"></a>
## func [GetIPWithMask](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/network.go#L88>)

```go
func GetIPWithMask(r *http.Request, options ...Options) net.IP
```

GetIPWithMask returns IP address from request by applying a mask. If options is defined and either TrustForwardHeader is true or ClientIPHeader is defined, it will lookup IP in HTTP headers. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="Context"></a>
## type [Context](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/limiter.go#L12-L17>)

Context is the limit context.

```go
type Context struct {
    Limit     int64
    Remaining int64
    Reset     int64
    Reached   bool
}
```

<a name="Limiter"></a>
## type [Limiter](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/limiter.go#L24-L28>)

Limiter is the limiter instance.

```go
type Limiter struct {
    Store   Store
    Rate    Rate
    Options Options
}
```

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) Get(ctx context.Context, key string) (Context, error)
```

<a name="Limiter.GetIP"></a>
### func \(\*Limiter\) [GetIP](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/network.go#L22>)

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) GetIP(r *http.Request) net.IP
```

GetIP returns IP address from request. If options is defined and either TrustForwardHeader is true or ClientIPHeader is defined, it will lookup IP in HTTP headers. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="Limiter.GetIPKey"></a>
### func \(\*Limiter\) [GetIPKey](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/network.go#L42>)

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) GetIPKey(r *http.Request) string
```

GetIPKey extracts IP from request and returns hashed IP to use as store key. If options is defined and either TrustForwardHeader is true or ClientIPHeader is defined, it will lookup IP in HTTP headers. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="Limiter.GetIPWithMask"></a>
### func \(\*Limiter\) [GetIPWithMask](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/network.go#L32>)

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) GetIPWithMask(r *http.Request) net.IP
```

GetIPWithMask returns IP address from request by applying a mask. If options is defined and either TrustForwardHeader is true or ClientIPHeader is defined, it will lookup IP in HTTP headers. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="Limiter.Increment"></a>
### func \(\*Limiter\) [Increment](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/limiter.go#L63>)

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) Increment(ctx context.Context, key string, count int64) (Context, error)
```

Increment increments the limit by given count & gives back the new limit for given identifier

<a name="Limiter.Peek"></a>
### func \(\*Limiter\) [Peek](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/limiter.go#L53>)

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) Peek(ctx context.Context, key string) (Context, error)
```

Peek returns the limit for given identifier, without modification on current values.

<a name="Limiter.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Limiter\) [Reset](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/limiter.go#L58>)

```go
func (limiter *Limiter) Reset(ctx context.Context, key string) (Context, error)
```

Reset sets the limit for given identifier to zero.

<a name="Option"></a>
## type [Option](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/options.go#L8>)

Option is a functional option.

```go
type Option func(*Options)
```

<a name="WithClientIPHeader"></a>
### func [WithClientIPHeader](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/options.go#L57>)

```go
func WithClientIPHeader(header string) Option
```

WithClientIPHeader will configure the limiter to use a custom header to obtain user IP. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="WithIPv4Mask"></a>
### func [WithIPv4Mask](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/options.go#L30>)

```go
func WithIPv4Mask(mask net.IPMask) Option
```

WithIPv4Mask will configure the limiter to use given mask for IPv4 address.

<a name="WithIPv6Mask"></a>
### func [WithIPv6Mask](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/options.go#L37>)

```go
func WithIPv6Mask(mask net.IPMask) Option
```

WithIPv6Mask will configure the limiter to use given mask for IPv6 address.

<a name="WithTrustForwardHeader"></a>
### func [WithTrustForwardHeader](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/options.go#L47>)

```go
func WithTrustForwardHeader(enable bool) Option
```

WithTrustForwardHeader will configure the limiter to trust X\-Real\-IP and X\-Forwarded\-For headers. Please be advised that using this option could be insecure \(ie: spoofed\) if your reverse proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP. Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.

<a name="Options"></a>
## type [Options](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/options.go#L11-L27>)

Options are limiter options.

```go
type Options struct {
    // IPv4Mask defines the mask used to obtain a IPv4 address.
    IPv4Mask net.IPMask
    // IPv6Mask defines the mask used to obtain a IPv6 address.
    IPv6Mask net.IPMask
    // TrustForwardHeader enable parsing of X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers to obtain user IP.
    // Please be advised that using this option could be insecure (ie: spoofed) if your reverse
    // proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP.
    // Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.
    TrustForwardHeader bool
    // ClientIPHeader defines a custom header (likely defined by your CDN or Cloud provider) to obtain user IP.
    // If configured, this option will override "TrustForwardHeader" option.
    // Please be advised that using this option could be insecure (ie: spoofed) if your reverse
    // proxy is not configured properly to forward a trustworthy client IP.
    // Please read the section "Limiter behind a reverse proxy" in the README for further information.
    ClientIPHeader string
}
```

## type [Rate](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/rate.go#L12-L16>)

```go
    Limit     int64
}
```

### func [NewRateFromFormatted](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/rate.go#L19>)

```go
func NewRateFromFormatted(formatted string) (Rate, error)
```

<a name="Store"></a>
## type [Store](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/store.go#L9-L18>)

Store is the common interface for limiter stores.

```go
type Store interface {
    // Get returns the limit for given identifier.
    Get(ctx context.Context, key string, rate Rate) (Context, error)
    // Peek returns the limit for given identifier, without modification on current values.
    Peek(ctx context.Context, key string, rate Rate) (Context, error)
    // Reset resets the limit to zero for given identifier.
    Reset(ctx context.Context, key string, rate Rate) (Context, error)
    // Increment increments the limit by given count & gives back the new limit for given identifier
    Increment(ctx context.Context, key string, count int64, rate Rate) (Context, error)
}
```

<a name="StoreOptions"></a>
## type [StoreOptions](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/store.go#L21-L34>)

StoreOptions are options for store.

```go
type StoreOptions struct {
    // Prefix is the prefix to use for the key.
    Prefix string

    // MaxRetry is the maximum number of retry under race conditions on redis store.
    // Deprecated: this option is no longer required since all operations are atomic now.
    MaxRetry int

    // CleanUpInterval is the interval for cleanup (run garbage collection) on stale entries on memory store.
    // Setting this to a low value will optimize memory consumption, but will likely
    // reduce performance and increase lock contention.
    // Setting this to a high value will maximum throughput, but will increase the memory footprint.
    CleanUpInterval time.Duration
}
```

# fasttime

```go
import "github.com/ulule/limiter/v3/internal/fasttime"
```

Package fasttime gets wallclock time, but super fast.

## Index

- [func Now\(\) uint64](<#Now>)


<a name="Now"></a>
## func [Now](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/internal/fasttime/fasttime.go#L18>)

```go
func Now() uint64
```

Now returns a monotonic clock value. The actual value will differ across systems, but that's okay because we generally only care about the deltas.

```go
func WithLimitReachedHandler(handler LimitReachedHandler) Option
```

WithLimitReachedHandler will configure the Middleware to use the given LimitReachedHandler.

```go
type ErrorHandler func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, err error)
```

<a name="Middleware"></a>
## type [Middleware](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/middleware/stdlib/middleware.go#L11-L17>)

Middleware is the middleware for basic http.Handler.

```go
type Middleware struct {
    Limiter        *limiter.Limiter
    OnError        ErrorHandler
    OnLimitReached LimitReachedHandler
    KeyGetter      KeyGetter
    ExcludedKey    func(string) bool
}
```

<a name="Cache"></a>
## type [Cache](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L114-L117>)

Cache contains a collection of counters.

```go
type Cache struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Cache.Clean"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Clean](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L226>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Clean()
```

Clean will deleted any expired keys.

<a name="Cache.Delete"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Delete](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L163>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Delete(key string)
```

Delete deletes the value for a key.

<a name="Cache.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Get](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L213>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Get(key string, duration time.Duration) (int64, time.Time)
```

Get returns key's value and expiration.

<a name="Cache.Increment"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Increment](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L186>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Increment(key string, value int64, duration time.Duration) (int64, time.Time)
```

Increment increments given value on key. If key is undefined or expired, it will create it.

<a name="Cache.Load"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Load](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L148>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Load(key string) (*Counter, bool)
```

Load returns the counter stored in the map for a key, or nil if no counter is present. The ok result indicates whether counter was found in the map.

<a name="Cache.LoadOrStore"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [LoadOrStore](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L136>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) LoadOrStore(key string, counter *Counter) (*Counter, bool)
```

LoadOrStore returns the existing counter for the key if present. Otherwise, it stores and returns the given counter. The loaded result is true if the counter was loaded, false if stored.

<a name="Cache.Range"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Range](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L169>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Range(handler func(key string, counter *Counter))
```

Range calls handler sequentially for each key and value present in the cache. If handler returns false, range stops the iteration.

<a name="Cache.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Reset](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L235>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Reset(key string, duration time.Duration) (int64, time.Time)
```

Reset changes the key's value and resets the expiration.

<a name="Cache.Store"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Store](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L158>)

```go
func (cache *Cache) Store(key string, counter *Counter)
```

Store sets the counter for a key.

<a name="CacheWrapper"></a>
## type [CacheWrapper](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L13-L15>)

CacheWrapper is used to ensure that the underlying cleaner goroutine used to clean expired keys will not prevent Cache from being garbage collected.

```go
type CacheWrapper struct {
    *Cache
}
```

<a name="NewCache"></a>
### func [NewCache](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L120>)

```go
func NewCache(cleanInterval time.Duration) *CacheWrapper
```

NewCache returns a new cache.

<a name="Counter"></a>
## type [Counter](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L55-L59>)

Counter is a simple counter with an expiration.

```go
type Counter struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Counter.Expiration"></a>
### func \(\*Counter\) [Expiration](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L69>)

```go
func (counter *Counter) Expiration() int64
```

Expiration returns the counter expiration.

<a name="Counter.Expired"></a>
### func \(\*Counter\) [Expired](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L76>)

```go
func (counter *Counter) Expired() bool
```

Expired returns true if the counter has expired.

<a name="Counter.Increment"></a>
### func \(\*Counter\) [Increment](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L99>)

```go
func (counter *Counter) Increment(value int64, expiration int64) (int64, int64)
```

Increment increments given value on this counter. If the counter is expired, it will use the given expiration. It returns its current value and expiration.

<a name="Counter.Load"></a>
### func \(\*Counter\) [Load](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L85>)

```go
func (counter *Counter) Load(expiration int64) (int64, int64)
```

Load returns the value and the expiration of this counter. If the counter is expired, it will use the given expiration.

<a name="Counter.Value"></a>
### func \(\*Counter\) [Value](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/cache.go#L62>)

```go
func (counter *Counter) Value() int64
```

Value returns the counter current value.

<a name="Store"></a>
## type [Store](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/store.go#L13-L18>)

Store is the in\-memory store.

```go
type Store struct {
    // Prefix used for the key.
    Prefix string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Store.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Get](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/store.go#L37>)

```go
func (store *Store) Get(ctx context.Context, key string, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Get returns the limit for given identifier.

<a name="Store.Increment"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Increment](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/store.go#L45>)

```go
func (store *Store) Increment(ctx context.Context, key string, count int64, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Increment increments the limit by given count & returns the new limit value for given identifier.

<a name="Store.Peek"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Peek](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/store.go#L53>)

```go
func (store *Store) Peek(ctx context.Context, key string, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Peek returns the limit for given identifier, without modification on current values.

<a name="Store.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Reset](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/memory/store.go#L61>)

```go
func (store *Store) Reset(ctx context.Context, key string, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Reset returns the limit for given identifier.


## func [NewStoreWithOptions](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L83>)

```go
func NewStoreWithOptions(client Client, options limiter.StoreOptions) (limiter.Store, error)
```

<a name="Client"></a>
## type [Client](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L44-L52>)

Client is an interface thats allows to use a redis cluster or a redis single client seamlessly.

```go
type Client interface {
    Get(ctx context.Context, key string) *libredis.StringCmd
    Set(ctx context.Context, key string, value interface{}, expiration time.Duration) *libredis.StatusCmd
    Watch(ctx context.Context, handler func(*libredis.Tx) error, keys ...string) error
    Del(ctx context.Context, keys ...string) *libredis.IntCmd
    SetNX(ctx context.Context, key string, value interface{}, expiration time.Duration) *libredis.BoolCmd
    EvalSha(ctx context.Context, sha string, keys []string, args ...interface{}) *libredis.Cmd
    ScriptLoad(ctx context.Context, script string) *libredis.StringCmd
}
```

<a name="Store"></a>
## type [Store](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L55-L71>)

Store is the redis store.

```go
type Store struct {
    // Prefix used for the key.
    Prefix string
    // MaxRetry is the maximum number of retry under race conditions.
    // Deprecated: this option is no longer required since all operations are atomic now.
    MaxRetry int
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Store.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Get](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L105>)

```go
func (store *Store) Get(ctx context.Context, key string, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Get returns the limit for given identifier.

<a name="Store.Increment"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Increment](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L99>)

```go
func (store *Store) Increment(ctx context.Context, key string, count int64, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Increment increments the limit by given count & gives back the new limit for given identifier

<a name="Store.Peek"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Peek](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L111>)

```go
func (store *Store) Peek(ctx context.Context, key string, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Peek returns the limit for given identifier, without modification on current values.

<a name="Store.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Reset](<https://github.com/ulule/limiter/blob/master/drivers/store/redis/store.go#L128>)

```go
func (store *Store) Reset(ctx context.Context, key string, rate limiter.Rate) (limiter.Context, error)
```

Reset returns the limit for given identifier which is set to zero.
